body:
{
  "title": "Reese Abebe — Head of Partnerships, Anchor Codec",
  "compiled_truth": "Reese Abebe joined [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) in April 2027 as Head of Partnerships, brought on specifically to build out the company's integration and distribution layer at a moment when the core product was stabilizing and the team needed someone who could translate technical capability into ecosystem reach. Before Anchor, Reese spent nearly a decade in business development and partnerships at infrastructure and cloud companies — the kind of roles where you're simultaneously managing a pipeline, negotiating API access terms, and trying to convince a platform team at a much larger org that your integration is worth prioritizing. That background made Reese a natural fit for the challenge at hand.

Reese is distribution-focused in a way that's almost philosophical. There's a running joke on the team that Reese doesn't believe a product exists until it's been embedded in someone else's workflow. That mindset has shaped how [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) approaches go-to-market: rather than chasing direct enterprise sales from day one, Reese pushed for a partnerships-first strategy — getting Anchor's single-pass primitives into the stacks of adjacent infrastructure providers, managed service platforms, and developer toolchains. By mid-2027, this was already showing results, with several signed integration agreements and a growing pipeline of co-marketing opportunties.

Collaborative to a fault, Reese is known internally for running an unusually open partnerships process. Deal memos get shared broadly, partner feedback gets piped directly into product channels, and Reese regularly pulls engineers into partner calls so they hear the pain points firsthand. This transparency occasionally creates friction — not everyone wants to sit through a 45-minute call about someone's ingestion pipeline — but it's built a level of alignment between partnerships and engineering that most startups at this stage don't have.

Before joining Anchor, Reese built integration ecosystems at two mid-stage infrastructure companies, one of which was aquired in 2025. That exit gave Reese both credibility and a network that proved immediately useful. Within weeks of joining, Reese had reactivated old relationships to set up introductory conversations with potential partners. The speed at which early deals materialized was, by most accounts, directly attributable to Reese's existing reputation in the infrastructure community.

Reese is based in the DC metro area and travels frequently to the Bay Area and New York for partner meetings.",
  "timeline": "- **2027-04-01** | Reese Abebe officially joins [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor) as Head of Partnerships.
- **2027-04-18** | First partner outreach sprint begins; Reese re-engages contacts from prior infrastructure roles to seed the pipeline.
- **2027-05-22** | Internal presentation: "Distribution as Product" — Reese lays out the partnerships-first GTM thesis to the full team.
- **2027-07-10** | First signed integration agreement with a managed service platform; Reese co-leads the announcement.
- **2027-08-15** | Reese introduces a shared deal memo process, giving engineering and product direct visibility into partner conversations.
- **2027-10-03** | Speaks on a panel at an infrastructure conference in New York about building integration ecosystems at early-stage companies.
- **2027-12-01** | End-of-year review shows four active integration partnerships and a pipeline of nine more in various stages.
- **2028-01-20** | Reese begins scoping a formal partner program with tiered integration levels and co-marketing commitments.
- **2028-04-08** | Anchor's partner program launches publicly; Reese authors a blog post outlining the philosophy behind it.
- **2028-06-12** | Quarterly board update credits partnerships channel with contributing roughly 30% of new inbound interest for [Anchor Codec](companies/anchor)."
}
